description_long: This is a single-propeller, military-style airplane with a streamlined fuselage and a low-mounted straight wing configuration. The aircraft is painted in a matte dark blue-gray color with white military insignia on the wings and fuselage. The cockpit is enclosed with a clear canopy, positioned just behind the propeller and ahead of the wing root. The tail section features a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, all painted in the same color scheme. The propeller at the nose is a classic four-blade design, and the overall shape suggests a World War II-era fighter. No landing gear is visible, indicating it may be retracted or not modeled.
description: A dark blue-gray, single-propeller military airplane with low-mounted straight wings, white insignia, and an enclosed cockpit.
description_view_0: Side view showing the streamlined fuselage, low-mounted wings, and visible propeller at the nose.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the propeller, narrow fuselage, and symmetrical wing placement.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, with clear visibility of the tail and wing insignia.
description_view_3: Top-down view displaying the full wingspan, cockpit canopy, and tail configuration.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, straight wings with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a four-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Landing gear not visible; likely retractable or not modeled.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed single-seat cockpit with a clear canopy.
